core_mechanism: Whitham modulation theory: averaging a nonlinear dispersive wave equation over its fast periodic oscillations to derive slowly-varying (hydrodynamic) modulation equations that govern the expanding oscillatory front — a dispersive shock — that develops from a step-like (Riemann) initial discontinuity
brief_reason: The abstract builds its analytical theory of dispersive shock waves on the Whitham modulation approach applied to a sharp step-like initial discontinuity, which is exactly the machinery of averaging over fast nonlinear oscillations to obtain modulation equations for a dispersive shock front.
